title: Firefighting drones in the works as wildfires plague US nearly year-round
excerpt: California and XPRIZE competition tests whether drones can stop wildfires early.
content_html:
Drones capable of spraying water and fire retardants have been practicing how to snuff out wildfires early in California and Alaska this summer—demonstrating a possible rapid-response tool for firefighting as climate change transforms wildfire season into a nearly year-round risk for much of the United States.

Most drones are significantly smaller than crewed aircraft and have shorter flight ranges, meaning they cannot replace large airtankers in delivering massive payloads of water or other fire-suppressing payloads to remote wildfires. But companies and fire agencies—along with organizers of the $11 million XPRIZE competition—are testing whether drones can help firefighting crews respond more quickly to small fires and put them out before they become more destructive.

The California Department of Forestry and Fire Protection, also known as CAL FIRE, ran its own field test involving five autonomous drones that worked together to deploy between 500 and 1,000 gallons of foam combined for suppressing fires on July 15, according to the TV station KPMH. The demonstration was organized with the help of the nonprofit FireWERX and the California-based company Seneca, which is making the drones commercially available starting in 2026.

Each of Seneca’s Argo-1 drones can carry about 100 pounds of water or fire retardant while working together in swarms of four to six drones. Once a human operator uploads a GPS waypoint, the autonomous drones fly toward their target and use onboard sensors to spot the heat signature of a fire, HeliOps Magazine reported. The drones then find the best hovering altitude before each lining up to spray the fire, one after the other.

The fully loaded drones are limited to a round trip of 10 miles while flying at about 30 miles per hour on average, which means they would ideally need to be prepositioned in fire-prone areas or first transported by ground vehicle. But each drone can fit in the back of a pickup truck with the tailgate down, and two people can even manually carry the empty drones.

blocks.html_en: Drones capable of spraying water and fire retardants have been practicing how to snuff out wildfires early in California and Alaska this summer—demonstrating a possible rapid-response tool for firefighting as climate change transforms wildfire season into a nearly year-round risk for much of the United States.

sentences.en: Drones capable of spraying water and fire retardants have been practicing how to snuff out wildfires early in California and Alaska this summer—demonstrating a possible rapid-response tool for firefighting as climate change transforms wildfire season into a nearly year-round risk for much of the United States.
sentences.zh: 今年夏天，能够喷洒水和阻燃剂的无人机一直在练习如何在加利福尼亚州和阿拉斯加尽早扑灭野火，这展示了一种可能的快速反应工具，因为气候变化使野火季节变成了美国大部分地区几乎全年的风险。

blocks.html_en: Most drones are significantly smaller than crewed aircraft and have shorter flight ranges, meaning they cannot replace large airtankers in delivering massive payloads of water or other fire-suppressing payloads to remote wildfires. But companies and fire agencies—along with organizers of the $11 million XPRIZE competition—are testing whether drones can help firefighting crews respond more quickly to small fires and put them out before they become more destructive.

sentences.en: Most drones are significantly smaller than crewed aircraft and have shorter flight ranges, meaning they cannot replace large airtankers in delivering massive payloads of water or other fire-suppressing payloads to remote wildfires.
sentences.zh: 大多数无人机比载人飞机小得多，飞行范围也更短，这意味着它们无法取代大型空中加油机向偏远的野火输送大量水或其他灭火有效载荷。

sentences.en: But companies and fire agencies—along with organizers of the $11 million XPRIZE competition—are testing whether drones can help firefighting crews respond more quickly to small fires and put them out before they become more destructive.
sentences.zh: 但公司和消防机构以及价值 1100 万美元的 XPRIZE 竞赛的组织者正在测试无人机是否可以帮助消防人员更快地应对小火，并在火势变得更具破坏性之前将其扑灭。

blocks.html_en: The California Department of Forestry and Fire Protection, also known as CAL FIRE, ran its own field test involving five autonomous drones that worked together to deploy between 500 and 1,000 gallons of foam combined for suppressing fires on July 15, according to the TV station KPMH. The demonstration was organized with the help of the nonprofit FireWERX and the California-based company Seneca, which is making the drones commercially available starting in 2026.

sentences.en: The California Department of Forestry and Fire Protection, also known as CAL FIRE, ran its own field test involving five autonomous drones that worked together to deploy between 500 and 1,000 gallons of foam combined for suppressing fires on July 15, according to the TV station KPMH.
sentences.zh: 据 KPMH 电视台报道，加州林业和消防局（也称为 CAL FIRE）于 7 月 15 日进行了自己的现场测试，涉及五架自动无人机，这些无人机协同部署了 500 至 1,000 加仑的泡沫来灭火。

sentences.en: The demonstration was organized with the help of the nonprofit FireWERX and the California-based company Seneca, which is making the drones commercially available starting in 2026.
sentences.zh: 此次演示是在非营利组织 FireWERX 和总部位于加州的 Seneca 公司的帮助下组织的，该公司将于 2026 年开始将无人机投入商业使用。

blocks.html_en: Each of Seneca’s Argo-1 drones can carry about 100 pounds of water or fire retardant while working together in swarms of four to six drones. Once a human operator uploads a GPS waypoint, the autonomous drones fly toward their target and use onboard sensors to spot the heat signature of a fire, HeliOps Magazine reported. The drones then find the best hovering altitude before each lining up to spray the fire, one after the other.

sentences.en: Each of Seneca’s Argo-1 drones can carry about 100 pounds of water or fire retardant while working together in swarms of four to six drones.
sentences.zh: 每架 Seneca 的 Argo-1 无人机可以携带约 100 磅的水或阻燃剂，同时以四到六架无人机的形式协同工作。

sentences.en: Once a human operator uploads a GPS waypoint, the autonomous drones fly toward their target and use onboard sensors to spot the heat signature of a fire, HeliOps Magazine reported.
sentences.zh: 据《HeliOps》杂志报道，一旦人类操作员上传 GPS 航路点，自主无人机就会飞向目标，并使用机载传感器来发现火灾的热信号。

sentences.en: The drones then find the best hovering altitude before each lining up to spray the fire, one after the other.
sentences.zh: 然后，无人机找到最佳的悬停高度，然后依次排队喷火。

blocks.html_en: The fully loaded drones are limited to a round trip of 10 miles while flying at about 30 miles per hour on average, which means they would ideally need to be prepositioned in fire-prone areas or first transported by ground vehicle. But each drone can fit in the back of a pickup truck with the tailgate down, and two people can even manually carry the empty drones.

sentences.en: The fully loaded drones are limited to a round trip of 10 miles while flying at about 30 miles per hour on average, which means they would ideally need to be prepositioned in fire-prone areas or first transported by ground vehicle.
sentences.zh: 满载无人机的往返飞行距离被限制为 10 英里，平均飞行速度约为每小时 30 英里，这意味着它们理想情况下需要预先部署在火灾多发地区或首先由地面车辆运输。

sentences.en: But each drone can fit in the back of a pickup truck with the tailgate down, and two people can even manually carry the empty drones.
sentences.zh: 但每架无人机都可以放在尾门放下的皮卡车后部，两个人甚至可以手动搬运空无人机。

content_html_zh:
今年夏天，能够喷洒水和阻燃剂的无人机一直在练习如何在加利福尼亚州和阿拉斯加尽早扑灭野火，这展示了一种可能的快速反应工具，因为气候变化使野火季节变成了美国大部分地区几乎全年的风险。

大多数无人机比载人飞机小得多，飞行范围也更短，这意味着它们无法取代大型空中加油机向偏远的野火输送大量水或其他灭火有效载荷。 但公司和消防机构以及价值 1100 万美元的 XPRIZE 竞赛的组织者正在测试无人机是否可以帮助消防人员更快地应对小火，并在火势变得更具破坏性之前将其扑灭。

据 KPMH 电视台报道，加州林业和消防局（也称为 CAL FIRE）于 7 月 15 日进行了自己的现场测试，涉及五架自动无人机，这些无人机协同部署了 500 至 1,000 加仑的泡沫来灭火。 此次演示是在非营利组织 FireWERX 和总部位于加州的 Seneca 公司的帮助下组织的，该公司将于 2026 年开始将无人机投入商业使用。

每架 Seneca 的 Argo-1 无人机可以携带约 100 磅的水或阻燃剂，同时以四到六架无人机的形式协同工作。 据《HeliOps》杂志报道，一旦人类操作员上传 GPS 航路点，自主无人机就会飞向目标，并使用机载传感器来发现火灾的热信号。 然后，无人机找到最佳的悬停高度，然后依次排队喷火。

满载无人机的往返飞行距离被限制为 10 英里，平均飞行速度约为每小时 30 英里，这意味着它们理想情况下需要预先部署在火灾多发地区或首先由地面车辆运输。 但每架无人机都可以放在尾门放下的皮卡车后部，两个人甚至可以手动搬运空无人机。
